host: darwin-arm64 (compile) -> ubu-2 RTX 5070 driver 580.126.09 + ptxas CUDA 12.0 (fire)

prereqs_main_head.n86_nvptx_target_recognizers: PRESENT (3 fns + dispatcher at compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa:4447,4463,4479,4500-4502 from commit 7f3448aa)
prereqs_main_head.n100_hir_to_mir_synthesis: PRESENT (gpu_matmul intercept at compiler/lower/hir_to_mir.hexa:1138-1169 from commit f2770d6e)

verdict: FAIL — N86 codegen has 3 distinct PTX-emit bugs blocking source-to-silicon closure

verification_passes.ptx_emit: PASS (driver wrote 4843 B sm_80 PTX, 4845 B sm_120 PTX, phase=P3-PATH-B)
verification_passes.ptxas_accept_original_emit: FAIL (line 101 wmma.mma 'Unexpected instruction types', line 110 wmma.store.d 'Arguments mismatch')
verification_passes.ptxas_accept_handfixed: PASS after 3 surgical edits (.shared->.global on load/store, mma type spec, store operand order) — 32 regs, 400 B cmem[0]
verification_passes.numeric_equivalence_handfixed: FAIL (max_abs=13.60, max_rel=1479; all 4096 cells nonzero but wrong values — suggests additional addressing/tiling bug in N86 emit)

found_with_wrong_modifier_order: wmma.load.a.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.f16.shared (token order .row before .m16n16k16 + .shared state-space at end is wrong-shape vs expected and ptx-invalid for global ptrs)

bug_1_wmma_load_state_space.issue: (a) state-space modifier must precede element-type, (b) state-space is .global (pointer is loaded from .param.u64 -> generic), not .shared
bug_1_wmma_load_state_space.ptxas_diag: (no diag emitted; ptxas accepted .shared but downstream wmma.mma rejects)

expected_either: wmma.mma.sync.aligned.row.col.m16n16k16.f32.f32 {D8}, {A8}, {B8}, {C8}  (short form, f16 a/b implicit via .b32 packing)
wmma.mma.sync.aligned.row.col.m16n16k16.f32.f16.f16.f32 {D4}, {A4}, {B4}, {C4}  (long form requires HALF-sized fragments per PTX 7.0 spec; only valid for the alternative 4-reg form)
bug_2_wmma_mma_type_spec.issue: Long form .f32.f16.f16.f32 is reserved for the 4-reg fragment layout (bf16/etc). With 8 .b32 A/B + 8 .f32 D/C the canonical mnemonic is the short form .f32.f32. Every other f16 m16n16k16 oracle PTX in inbox/fires/ uses .f32.f32. The .f32.bf16.bf16.f32 form is the long form for bf16; .f16.f16 is not valid as input-type spec.

bug_3_wmma_store_d.issue: (a) state-space must precede element-type (same as bug 1), (b) state-space is .global not .shared, (c) operand order: address first then fragment, not fragment then address

bug_4_likely_addressing_or_tiling.evidence: hand-fixed PTX (3 surgical fixes above) compiles + runs but max_abs=13.6 / max_rel=1479; all 4096 cells nonzero but values don't match CPU reference. Pre-transposing B to col-major in the host also fails (max_abs=12.7).
bug_4_likely_addressing_or_tiling.suspect: Address computation for A/B per-tile likely correct (single warp per (M/16,N/16) block, K-loop kk += 16 with stride %r5/%r6) but the .col B-load expects column-major B in memory while the test fixture treats B as row-major. Other suspects: stride encoding mismatch (param %r6 may need byte-stride not element-stride), or wmma fragment-layout convention mismatch between row-major A load + col-major B load.
bug_4_likely_addressing_or_tiling.ptxas_diag: n/a (PTX compiles after bugs 1-3 fix, but kernel produces incorrect output)

source_to_silicon_e2e_closure_status: STILL BLOCKED — driver-emitted PTX is rejected by ptxas; hand-fix of 3 mnemonic-shape bugs gets the kernel to launch but reveals a 4th addressing/layout bug. Source-to-silicon path is wired (HIR -> MIR -> NVPTX dispatcher -> 5/5 expected WMMA concepts emitted) but the emit STRINGS themselves are malformed against PTX 7.0 grammar.

honest_scope_per_g3.compiler_source_untouched: TRUE per task constraint (DO NOT touch compiler source)
honest_scope_per_g3.scope_compliance: TRUE per task brief honest-scope clause 'If WMMA emit has bug (shape mismatch), document precisely'
honest_scope_per_g3.not_claiming: Source-to-silicon matmul E2E CLOSED — counter-falsified by ptxas + numeric. Both N86 (PTX-shape emit) AND a follow-up tiling/layout bug must be fixed before re-fire.

remediation_next_cycle.bug_1_3_fix_location: compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa — _nvptx_emit_matmul_body and related helpers (lines around 4400-4520). Change mnemonic emission to: load = 'wmma.load.a.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.global.f16', load b = 'wmma.load.b.sync.aligned.col.m16n16k16.global.f16', store = 'wmma.store.d.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.global.f32 [addr], {regs}, stride'
remediation_next_cycle.bug_2_fix: Same file — use short form 'wmma.mma.sync.aligned.row.col.m16n16k16.f32.f32' (the standard for the 8-b32-A + 8-b32-B + 8-f32-D + 8-f32-C layout). The .f32.f16.f16.f32 long form requires the 4-reg fragment layout (different codegen).
remediation_next_cycle.bug_4_fix: Address/stride computation in K-loop. Audit row-major A vs col-major B layout assumption against test fixture's layout; consider switching B load to .row and accepting row-major B (the canonical layout for nn.Linear weight typically). Also audit whether stride argument is element-count or byte-count for wmma.load (it is element-count per PTX 7.0 spec).
remediation_next_cycle.oracle_reference: inbox/fires/rfc067_p4_2026_05_20/wmma_16x16.ptx — 67-line working f16 m16n16k16 oracle, ptxas-accepted, single-tile (M=N=K=16). Use as the byte-eq target for N86 emit.
